original_abstract:
In this study, the chemical profiles and biological activities of supercritical carbon dioxide extracts from the peels of two pitaya varieties, *Hylocereus polyrhizus* and *Hylocereus undatus*, were investigated. Using gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS) analysis, 24 compounds were identified in *H. polyrhizus* and 19 in *H. undatus*, representing 90.66% and 92.82% of the total compositions, respectively. Both extracts were primarily composed of triterpenoids and steroids; notably, *H. polyrhizus* was characterized by high concentrations of $\beta$-amyrin (15.87%), $\alpha$-amyrin (13.90%), and octacosane (12.2%), while *H. undatus* was dominated by $\beta$-amyrin (23.39%) and $\gamma$-sitosterol (19.32%). 

The therapeutic potential of these extracts was evaluated through *in vitro* cytotoxic and antioxidant assays. Both extracts demonstrated significant dose-dependent growth inhibition against three human cancer cell lines: PC3 (prostate), Bcap-37 (breast), and MGC-803 (gastric), with $IC_{50}$ values ranging from 0.43 to 0.73 mg/mL. Follow-up testing of major isolated components suggested that $\beta$-amyrin, $\beta$-sitosterol, and stigmast-4-en-3-one contribute substantially to this cytotoxicity. Additionally, the extracts exhibited radical scavenging activity in DPPH assays, yielding $IC_{50}$ values of 0.83 mg/mL (*H. polyrhizus*) and 0.91 mg/mL (*H. undatus*). These findings suggest that pitaya peel, often discarded as agricultural waste, serves as a rich source of bioactive compounds with potential applications in the nutraceutical and pharmaceutical industries.
humanized_abstract:
Supercritical carbon dioxide (SC-CO2) extracts from the peels of two different pitaya cultivars, *Hylocereus polyrhizus* and *Hylocereus undatus*, were studied using GC-MS (Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry) to analyze the chemical profiles of these extracts and assess their biological activities. The results indicated that there were 24 and 19 types of compounds in the SC-CO2 extracts from *H. polyrhizus* and *H. undatus*, respectively, accounting for 90.66% and 92.82% of the composition. The main compositions of the SC-CO2 extracts were triterpenoids and steroids. In particular, the content of $\beta$-amyrin, $\alpha$-amyrin and octacosane in the SC-CO2 extract from *H. polyrhizus* peel reached 15.87%, 13.90% and 12.2%, respectively. Meanwhile, the content of $\beta$-amyrin and $\gamma$-sitosterol in the SC-CO2 extract from *H. undatus* peel peal reached 23.39% and 19.32%, respectively.

*Extracts from Pitaya Peels show Cytotoxic and Antioxidant Activities* The anticancer effect of the plant extracts was confirmed through *in vitro* cytotoxic and antioxidant assays. Results showed a high degree of dose-dependent growth inhibition towards three human cancer cell lines, namely PC3 (prostate cancer), Bcap-37 (breast cancer) and MGC-803 (gastric cancer) with $IC_{50}$ values ranging from 0.43 to 0.73 mg/mL for both extracts. A follow-up analysis of the major components of the extracts suggested that *β*-amyrin, *β*-sitosterol and stigmast-4-en-3-one were the contributors to their cytotoxic effects. The antioxidant capacity of the extracts was confirmed via their radical scavenging abilities against DPPH with $IC_{50}$ values of 0.83 mg/mL for *H. polyrhizus* extract and 0.91 mg/mL for *H. undatus* extract. The pitaya peel, a by-product of the agroindustry, was identified for the first time as a readily available plant material for development of a nutritional and pharmaceutical resource.

document.output:
Supercritical carbon dioxide (SC-CO2) extracts from the peels of two different pitaya cultivars, *Hylocereus polyrhizus* and *Hylocereus undatus*, were studied using GC-MS (Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry) to analyze the chemical profiles of these extracts and assess their biological activities. The results indicated that there were 24 and 19 types of compounds in the SC-CO2 extracts from *H. polyrhizus* and *H. undatus*, respectively, accounting for 90.66% and 92.82% of the composition. The main compositions of the SC-CO2 extracts were triterpenoids and steroids. In particular, the content of $\beta$-amyrin, $\alpha$-amyrin and octacosane in the SC-CO2 extract from *H. polyrhizus* peel reached 15.87%, 13.90% and 12.2%, respectively. The antioxidant capacity of the extracts was confirmed via their radical scavenging abilities against DPPH with $IC_{50}$ values of 0.83 mg/mL for *H. polyrhizus* extract and 0.91 mg/mL for *H. undatus* extract. In this study, the chemical profiles and biological activities of supercritical carbon dioxide extracts from the peels of two pitaya varieties, *Hylocereus polyrhizus* and *Hylocereus undatus*, were investigated.
